turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Participating in Group Decisions and Negotiating Responsibilities

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi everyone. Before we start anything, I think we really need to talk about how we are going to work together, because last time in another subject it became very stressful at the end.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I think that’s a good idea. Group work can get messy if roles aren’t clear. What part do you want to start with?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe roles first. Because if roles are unclear, then people either do nothing or do too much.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s true. What kind of role do you think suits you best?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think I can do research. I’m okay with reading articles, finding sources, and summarising information.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s great. How do you feel about presenting or speaking in front of the class?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Honestly, I feel nervous about that. I can speak, but when I’m nervous, my English becomes worse.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s understandable. Would you rather focus on writing instead of presenting?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, writing is more comfortable. But… I don’t want to avoid presenting completely, because I know it’s important.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So maybe a smaller speaking role?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, maybe. Like explaining one slide, not the whole presentation.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds reasonable. We can plan it that way.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. But writing also has many parts, so I want to be clear which writing part I will do.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good point. We have introduction, literature review, analysis, and conclusion. Which part would you prefer?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Literature review. But I want to check if I understand correctly.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, go ahead.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Literature review means summarising what other researchers said, not my opinion, right?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Mostly yes. You summarise and compare previous studies, and maybe connect them to our topic.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, so some connection is allowed. I thought it’s only summary.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re right to check. It’s not only summary.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Alright. I think I can do that.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: How about deadlines? Do you think we should set internal deadlines?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, definitely. If we don’t, everything will be last minute.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What would you suggest?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe one week before the actual submission. But I’m not sure if that’s realistic for everyone.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s a fair concern. Why do you think it might be difficult?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Because some people work part-time, and sometimes they reply late. I don’t want to sound controlling.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s understandable. How could we phrase it so it feels supportive, not controlling?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe we can say “draft deadline”, not “final deadline”.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s a good strategy.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Also, I think regular check-ins help. Not long meetings, just short messages.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: How often do you think is reasonable?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe every three days. But not too strict.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So something flexible but consistent.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. Because English discussion meetings are tiring for me, honestly.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s completely fair. We can keep communication simple.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, so let me summarise to check if I understood correctly. My role is research and literature review writing, small speaking part in presentation, draft deadline one week early, and short check-ins every few days.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that matches what we discussed.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. I feel much less anxious now.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I’m glad. Clear planning really helps.
